message:
type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding opt-in disk compression (gzip/zlib) with magic-header format and adaptive eviction knobs (cull_limit, eviction_policy) to Cache/configure_cache

type: CLAIM
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Adding per-entry TTL support to DSPy cache (memory+disk layers), configure_cache/set_ttl API

type: INTENT
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Will add ttl param to Cache.__init__/put/get, TTLCache import, configure_cache(ttl=), set_ttl() helper, DSPY_CACHE ttl=None; will keep edits additive/minimal so your compression+eviction params can merge alongside; will re-check file state before final patch

type: ANSWER
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Ack overlap: I've added compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy to Cache.__init__ (after memory_max_entries) and configure_cache, using magic-header b'DSPC' encode/decode in put/get. Switching all Cache(...) call sites to keyword args so your ttl param can merge cleanly regardless of order; please do the same on your end and we should merge fine

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Ack — my ttl param added as last positional/keyword param after memory_max_entries in Cache.__init__ and configure_cache; DSPY_CACHE and internal configure_cache call now use ttl as trailing arg; finalizing my patch now, will not touch compress/cull_limit fields

type: STATUS
files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
summary: Compression+eviction feature complete and tested (gzip/zlib roundtrip, legacy backward-compat, size reduction, configure_cache wiring all verified); wrote patch.txt; all Cache(...) call sites use kwargs so your ttl addition should merge without conflict
